findings.message: plugin.json version 5.1.0 does not match package.json version 1.0.0; the plugin's own enhance-plugins skill flags this as a HIGH-certainty structural bug.
plugin.json version 5.1.0 does not match package.json version 1.0.0; the plugin's own schema requires these to be in sync.
The 8 Critical Rules list mandates without rationale; rules without WHY explanations have lower compliance rates.
Agent has no examples block; zero examples in an agent definition reduces reliability for edge cases and reduces test coverage.
Agent body contains no output format specification; output format is entirely delegated to the skill with no fallback description in the agent.
All 8 enhancer agents advertise --fix apply behaviour but declare no Write or Edit tools; if skills cannot bypass agent tool restrictions, auto-fix is silently broken across all agents.
Agent has no examples block.
Agent body contains no output format specification.
Description uses 'better AI understanding' — a vague outcome claim that does not specify what constitutes 'better'.
Agent has no examples block.
Agent body contains no output format specification.
'Structural analysis (no complex reasoning needed)' — 'complex' is vague and context-dependent.

'Balance AI optimization with human readability' — 'balance' does not specify a measurable trade-off point.

'Be cautious about security patterns' — 'cautious' gives no measurable direction.

Agent has no examples block.
Agent body contains no output format specification.
'Consider skill context when evaluating trigger quality' — 'consider' is a weak directive.
Command frontmatter is missing the allowed-tools field; Claude Code cannot determine which tools to pre-authorise when dispatching the command.

Binary agent-analyzer is fetched from GitHub releases at runtime via HTTPS on first use. SHA-256 sidecar and optional SLSA attestation provide integrity checks, but a stolen release token plus matching SHA-256 sidecar would bypass the checksum gate.

SLSA attestation is soft-fail by default: if the gh CLI is absent, verification is skipped with a warning. Only SHA-256 protection remains, which does not close the stolen-release-token attack vector.
'Positioning suggestions are HIGH certainty but may have valid exceptions' — 'may have' is a hedge that reduces the prescriptive value of the guidance.

Four vague terms in detection patterns: 'complex logic', 'simple and fast', 'unreasonably long timeouts', used without quantification — conflicts with the skill's own requirement for precise pattern matching.
'Limit to 3-5 relevant tools' uses 'relevant'; 'Consider dynamic tool loading for large toolsets' uses 'consider' and 'large' without thresholds.
'Consider skill context when evaluating trigger quality' and 'Missing argument-hint for skills that clearly need input' use 'consider' and 'clearly' without measurable criteria.
Command frontmatter is missing the allowed-tools field; Claude Code cannot determine which tools to pre-authorise when dispatching the command.
plugin.json version 5.1.0 does not match package.json version 1.0.0; the plugin's own enhance-plugins skill flags this as a HIGH-certainty structural bug.
